title: Internal Complaints (2010 - Present) (Sheriff)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period from January 1, 2010 to the date this request was filed, please provide a spreadsheet of all complaints against officers in CSV or Excel format with only the following columns: Investigation/Complaint No., Incident Type, Occurred Date, Complaint Reported On Date, Employee Badge Number, Employee First Name, Employee Last Name, Allegation, Allegation: Policy/Directive Violated, Allegation: Finding, Disposition, Discipline Imposed.

Please provide all complaints, regardless of severity,in cluding any complaints that may not have been classified for full investigation and were referred to an employee's supervisor for counseling or retraining as well as complaints that were investigated by an employee's command instead of the normal internal affairs process. I would ask that you fulfill my request in a single installment in one spreadsheet instead of splitting it up into multiple annual spreadsheets if possible.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

title: UConn Spending on Holiday Decorations and Related Electricity Costs

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: UConn Spending on Holiday Decorations and Related Electricity Costs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the period November 23 through January 1 for each year from 2021-2025, across all University of Connecticut campuses:
1. Any and all records, invoices, purchase orders, receipts, memos, and reports related to the purchase of holiday decorations and holiday lighting.
2. Any and all records, invoices, receipts, memos, emails, correspondence,  and reports related to the cost of holiday lighting installation. 
3. Any and all records, invoices, purchase orders, receipts, memos, emails, correspondence, and reports related to estimated electricity costs associated with illuminating holiday lighting.
4. Any and all records, invoices, memos, emails, correspondence, and reports related to the daily operating hours for holiday lighting, including how long lighting is illuminated each day.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Julianna D'Addona

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Tennessee HHS response refile
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Tennessee Department of Children's Services remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request communications to or from Tennessee Department of Children's Services employees from Aug. 26, 2025, to date of search containing any of the following keywords: "PREP", "Personal Responsibility Education Program", "ACF", "Administration for Children and Families", "Teen Outreach Program", "TOP", and "Manhood 2.0".  

If the PREP curricula changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Ileana Garnand.

title: Obama Presidential Library: Records Concerning Analysis of Russian Data Localization Law and Implications for Chinese Cyber Policy

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: Records Concerning Analysis of Russian Data Localization Law and Implications for Chinese Cyber Policy
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to records created or received by officials within the National Security Council and related Executive Office of the President components during the administration of Barack Obama concerning analysis of Russia’s data localization regime and its potential implications for internet governance policies in other countries, particularly the People’s Republic of China.

Time Frame: September 1st 2014 to January 20th 2017

This timeframe captures the period following the passage and implementation of Russia’s data localization law and the subsequent blocking of the professional networking platform LinkedIn by the Russian government in 2016.

Offices Likely to Hold Responsive Records

Please search, at minimum, the files of the following components and staff:

National Security Council (NSC) Directorate for East Asian Affairs
National Security Council Directorate for Russia / Eurasia
NSC Directorate for Cybersecurity Coordination
NSC Directorate for International Economics
Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP)
Office of the U.S. Trade Representative (records maintained within EOP systems)

Key personnel whose files may contain responsive material includes, but is not limited to:

Ben Rhodes (likely email address of: Rhodes@nss.eop.gov and/or Rhodes@nsc.eop.gov and/or brhodes@nsc.eop.gov)
Michael Daniel (likely email address of: mdaniel@nsc.eop.gov)
Daniel Russel (likely email address of: RusselDR@state.gov)
Rob Malley (if cyber-policy discussions intersected broader strategy memos) - (likely email address of: rmalley@nsc.eop.gov or rmalley@nss.eop.gov)

Description of Requested Records

I request copies of memoranda, briefing papers, information papers, strategy papers, talking points, email correspondence, or policy analyses that:

Discuss or analyze Russia’s data localization law, specifically Federal Law No. 242-FZ.
Reference the Russian government’s decision to block LinkedIn within the Russian Federation in November 2016.
Evaluate whether Russia’s implementation of data localization or internet blocking policies could serve as a precedent, model, or “blueprint” for other governments seeking to assert digital sovereignty.
Compare Russian internet governance measures with Chinese cyber regulatory frameworks, including:

Cybersecurity Law of the People's Republic of China
China’s Multi-Level Protection Scheme (MLPS)
Broader Chinese concepts of “cyber sovereignty" or "digital sovereignty."

Assess whether Russia’s actions might contribute to:
fragmentation of the global internet
expansion of national data localization regimes
the emergence of a “splinternet.”

Search Terms

To assist archival searches, responsive records may include the following terms:

“LinkedIn Russia block”

“242-FZ”

“data localization Russia”

“precedent for China”

“cyber sovereignty”

"Russian digital sovereignty"

"Chinese digital sovereignty"

“MLPS” or “Multi-Level Protection Scheme”

“splinternet”

“digital trade barriers”

Types of Documents Requested

Please include, but do not limit searches to:

NSC Information Papers
NSC Decision Memos or Deputies Committee briefing materials
interagency policy coordination emails
internal talking points prepared for senior officials

Historical Significance

This request seeks to illuminate how the administration of Barack Obama evaluated the geopolitical implications of emerging digital sovereignty regimes following the events surrounding the Annexation of Crimea by the Russian Federation. In particular, it seeks records reflecting whether policymakers believed Russia’s enforcement actions against foreign internet companies would encourage similar policies in China and other states.

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

Thank you for the Obama Library's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Obama Presidential Library: U.S. Government/LinkedIn Correspondence Regarding Russian Law 242-FZ (2015-2017)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Obama Presidential Library: U.S. Government/LinkedIn Correspondence Regarding Russian Law 242-FZ (2015-2017)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Access to and copies of all records maintained by the Barack Obama Presidential Library related to correspondence between the U.S. Government and LinkedIn Corporation concerning Russian Federal Law No. 242-FZ (Data Localization Law).

I. Scope of Search
The timeframe for this request from September 1st, 2015 until noon on January 20th, 2017. This request includes, but is not limited to, email correspondence, audio recordings, video recordings, PowerPoint Presentations, briefing memos, meeting minutes, and diplomatic cables.

II. Targeted Offices & Agencies
Please search the following record groups within the Obama Administration:

National Security Council (NSC): Specifically the Directorate for Russia and Central Asia and the Directorate for Cyber Policy.

Department of Commerce: Specifically the Office of the Secretary and the International Trade Administration (ITA) (Office of Digital Services Industries).

Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR).

III. Targeted Domains and Entities
I request that a search be conducted for correspondence involving the following email domains and specific high-level executives:

Domains: @linkedin.com, @microsoft.com (post-June 2016).

LinkedIn Executives:

Jeff Weiner (CEO) 
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: jweiner@linkedin.com, jeff.weiner@linkedin.com, jeffweiner@linkedin.com, j.weiner@linkedin.com)

Erika Rottenberg (General Counsel/VP)
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: erottenberg@linkedin.com, erika.rottenberg@linkedin.com, erottenberg@linkedin.com, erika_rottenberg@linkedin.com)

Pablo Chavez (VP, Global Public Policy and Government Affairs)
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: pchavez@linkedin.com, pablo.chavez@linkedin.com, pabloc@linkedin.com, p.chavez@linkedin.com)

Nicole Wong (Former Deputy U.S. CTO with known ties to LinkedIn policy)
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: nicolew@linkedin.com, Nicole.wong@linkedin.com, nicole_wong@linkedin.com, nicoleawong@linkedin.com, nwong@linkedin.com)

Microsoft Executives:

Brad Smith (President and Chief Legal Office)
(Requested search against the following likely email addresses: brad.smith@microsoft.com, bsmith@microsoft.com, brads@microsoft.com)

John Frank (in late 2016 was Microsoft's VP of EU Government Affairs; often handled "Export" policy issues)
(Request search against the following likely email addresses: john.frank@microsoft.com, jfrank@microsoft.com, johnf@microsoft.com)

Dominic Carr (Microsoft's Head of Public Affairs in late 2016)
Request search against the following likely email addresses: dominic.carr@microsoft.com, dcarr@microsoft.com, domc@microsoft.com)


IV. Keywords & Search Terms
To facilitate a thorough search, please utilize the following keywords:

"LinkedIn", "Roskomnadzor", "242-FZ", "Data Localization", "Moscow City Court", "Tagansky District", "Severance", "Digital Trade Barrier", "Safe Harbor", "Privacy Shield", "Data Sovereignty", "IP Blocking".


This request seeks to clarify the "technical advice" and "compliance pathways" suggested by the U.S. government to private tech entities facing foreign regulatory pressure. It is of significant public interest to understand whether the Obama administration encouraged a policy of non-compliance as a form of digital protest or if technical workarounds (such as "mirroring" or "sharding" data) were deemed technically unfeasible or legally insufficient under Russian law.

Thank you for the Obama Library's commitment to transparency.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

Under the provisions of the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL), N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 84–90, I hereby request access to and copies of records held by the Town of Pine Plains, Dutchess County, relating to the planning, proposal, procurement, authorization, or attempted deployment of Flock Safety surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems.

This request is made in part due to reporting concerning the Pine Plains surveillance camera proposal:

https://www.timesunion.com/hudsonvalley/news/article/pine-plains-flock-surveillance-cameras-outcry-21958221.php

The purpose of this request is to determine the origin, authorization process, procurement activity, proposed locations, and communications surrounding the surveillance camera system, including identifying which individual, agency, or vendor first initiated discussions or planning for the program.

Timeframe: January 1, 2023 – Present, including any earlier records referencing or relating to Flock Safety, automated license plate readers, or surveillance cameras within the Town of Pine Plains.

Records Requested

For the purpose of this request, the records I seek include, but are not limited to the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements

All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, vendor agreements, procurement documents, or proposals involving the Town of Pine Plains and Flock Safety or any related entities concerning surveillance cameras, automated license plate readers, or related monitoring technology.

This includes draft contracts, executed agreements, amendments, pilot program agreements, trial program documentation, cancellation notices, procurement justifications, and contract termination records.

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system  
• the first communications between the Town and Flock Safety  
• vendor outreach to town officials  
• internal discussions about introducing the technology  
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors  

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Town Supervisor  
• Town Board members  
• Pine Plains Police Department personnel  
• Town administrative staff  
• Town legal counsel  
• Highway or Building Department personnel  
• other municipal offices  

that discuss:

• Flock Safety  
• automated license plate readers  
• surveillance cameras  
• camera deployments  
• vendor procurement or negotiations  
• surveillance technology adoption  

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the Town of Pine Plains and:

• representatives of Flock Safety  
• technology vendors or consultants  
• Dutchess County officials  
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies  
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers  

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks  
• vendor proposals  
• demonstration scheduling emails  
• meeting invitations or call summaries  
• webinar invitations  
• follow-up communications  

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the Town of Pine Plains.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements  
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps  
• deployment plans  
• proposed installation locations or intersections  
• roadway monitoring points  
• entry or exit routes to the town  
• communications discussing site selection  
• vendor recommendations for placement  
• field surveys or site inspections  
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement  

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans  
• camera location maps  
• infrastructure assessments  
• equipment specifications  
• installation schedules  
• operational capability descriptions  
• technology architecture documents  

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices  
• payment records  
• purchase authorizations  
• budget allocations  
• grant applications  
• cost analyses  
• procurement justifications  
• financial approvals  

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from Town Board meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where the Flock Safety system or surveillance camera program was discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to the surveillance camera program or procurement process.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data  
• policies governing license plate reader searches  
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks  
• data retention policies  
• privacy or civil liberties assessments  

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information  
• CC and BCC fields  
• full email header routing data  
• timestamps for sending and receipt  
• message ID fields  
• attachment metadata  
• document creation dates  
• last modified dates  
• author fields  
• file path information where available  

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter  
Independent Investigative Journalist

title: Hopsital discharge data (Pennsylvania Department of Health)

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Hopsital discharge data (Pennsylvania Department of Health)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hospital discharge data from January 1, 2023 to January 1, 2025. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: DOH - RTKL
communications.communication:
The Pennsylvania Department of Health's Right-to-Know Law (RTKL) Office has received your correspondence. Requests for records will be processed in accordance with the provisions of the RTKL, 65 P.S.§§67.101, et seq.

The Department is deemed to have received the request on the business day that the AORO actually receives the request. See 65 P.S. § 67.901. This includes after regular business hours (5 pm) or when the Office is otherwise closed pursuant to Management Directive 530.17 and will be marked as received on the next business day.
